intent: POSITIVE FIXTURE, THE MANDATORY NO-POLICY OFFLINE FLOOR PATH: explicitly a no-policy fixture. No binary policy is supplied; the expected output is the record-only floor (heuristic labels + a narrow allow WITH a caveat). Proves the always-on offline path works with zero policy input (offline cross-host analysis is core - spec). The denial is a realistic mislabeled-log case (logrotate_t reading var_log_t).
_kinds_note: floor=none: scontext (system_r, s0) and tcontext (object_r, s0) share the LEVEL (s0); the floor does NOT treat object_r (the universal object role) as a role mismatch (flagging it would false-flag every file denial). permissive=0, so no Permissive flag. The floor therefore falls through to its default: SUGGEST the narrow allow + a caveat. authoritative_category is intentionally NOT computed (no policy) - the value of this fixture is the no-policy PATH and the mandatory caveat, not a verified category.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow logrotate_t var_log_t:file { getattr read };  (matches expected.te; -N is itself a no-reference-policy raw baseline)
oracle.audit2allow_R: see audit2allow-R.te (a -R run was captured against stock policy for reference, but this fixture's contract is the NO-POLICY floor path)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: expected.te is the exact perm union { getattr read } on one triple; -R would expand to a logging_* interface adding perm padding + possibly a dir grant. expected.te omits all of that.
oracle.audit2why_detail: NO-POLICY fixture: audit2why intentionally not run. Without -p and without a live /sys/fs/selinux/policy (a --rm container has none), audit2why cannot replay - which is exactly the offline condition this fixture documents. The floor must operate from the record alone.

triage_expectation.caveat_required: Without a policy, the floor CANNOT rule out a constraint/bounds violation (it cannot distinguish this from scenario 41). The suggested allow MUST carry a caveat: 'suggested from the record alone; supply --policy to confirm this is a missing TE allow and not a constraint/bounds denial'.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl might (a) require a policy and refuse to run at all (breaking the mandatory offline path), or (b) emit the allow with NO caveat (over-confident - it has not ruled out a constraint). The correct behavior is suggest-with-caveat.
